response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically strong full-stack engineer with 15+ years of experience and an impressive track record at top-tier companies, but he fails to meet two hard requirements for this role: German language proficiency (he self-reports 'Basic,' whereas the role demands C1 minimum) and Python (not listed among his skills despite being a desired technical skill). Beyond those gaps, the role is fundamentally a leadership and client-facing architecture position at an IT consulting firm, yet the resume reads almost entirely as that of an individual contributor — there is no evidence of project management ownership, formal architectural deliverables, UML work, requirements management, or client advisory experience. The teaching and open-source contributions show initiative and communication ability, but they do not substitute for the operational and client-leadership track record this position requires.
strengths: Exceptionally broad full-stack experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and high-performance systems across 15+ years and multiple senior/staff-level roles at Google, Meta, and others
Demonstrated open-source commitment and Linux familiarity, including hosting infrastructure (GitLab, CI/CD, Docker, Kubernetes) and public GIS platform development
Strong knowledge-sharing history — organized public workshops, gave conference talks, and ran Selenium training courses — suggesting communication and mentoring capability
concerns: German is self-rated 'Basic,' directly failing the C1 minimum hard requirement for a German-market consulting role with direct client interaction
Resume reflects an individual-contributor career with no evidence of project management ownership, client advisory work, formal architecture deliverables, or team leadership responsibilities
Python and UML are absent from the skills section and nowhere implied in the work history, despite both being listed as desired/required for this role
